title: OpenAI is teasing new hardware… for Codex
slides: What if OpenAI's next move isn't a model?
They're building a dedicated chip for Codex.
Early tests show it runs 10x faster in inference.
Your coding assistant could become nearly instant.
Try the dev preview today and watch latency drop.
thread_text:
1. What if chips replace models?

OpenAI isn’t releasing a new model — they’re building a dedicated chip for Codex that runs inference 10x faster.

2. Latency drops below human reaction time.

Early tests show your coding assistant could become nearly instant — no more waiting for suggestions to load.

3. This isn’t a software update.

The chip is purpose-built for AI code generation, redefining how assistants perform at scale.

4. Your workflow changes completely.

Instant inference means you iterate mid-thought, not after a pause — a new mode of coding.

5. Try the dev preview today.

Watch latency drop in your own editor. Does waiting half a second still feel fast after you’ve seen instant?
video_script: OpenAI is building a dedicated chip for Codex.
Early tests show ten times faster inference.
Your coding assistant becomes nearly instant.
This changes how you iterate on code.
Try the dev preview and see latency vanish.

script: OpenAI is building a dedicated chip for Codex.
Early tests show ten times faster inference.
Your coding assistant becomes nearly instant.
This changes how you iterate on code.

instagram.caption:
They built a dedicated chip for Codex that runs inference 10x faster — not a new model. 🧠

Early tests show latency dropping below human reaction time. That means your coding assistant responds mid-thought, not after a pause. No more waiting for suggestions to load.

Your entire workflow shifts: you iterate instantly instead of stopping for half a second. Code generation becomes almost conversational.

Try the dev preview in your editor today — does that half-second wait still feel fast after you see instant?

carousel_slides.description: OpenAI is building a dedicated chip for Codex, not a new model. Inference runs 10x faster.
Your coding assistant could become nearly instant — no more waiting for suggestions to load.
The chip is purpose-built for AI code generation, redefining performance at scale.
Instant inference means you iterate mid-thought, not after a pause — a new coding mode.
Watch latency drop in your own editor. Click the link in our bio to start.
